The fault of course does not lie with astrology. It
shows our own imperfections and how we have not cared to
tap the original source of astrological knowledge, by a
proper study and adaptation of which it would doubtless be
posible to anticipate the exact type of education that one is
likely to take to either for his livelihood or for the sake of
knowledge. We are giving general combinations and a
number of illustrations leaving the reader to pursue his own
line of thought or argument to arrive at finer conclusions.
From the classical works are culled out the following
combinations indicative of inclinations towards different
branches of leaning.

Concerning the Fourth House 185
Venus in the fourth makes one proficient in music.
Mercury in the fourth makes one proficient in astrology.
The Sun and the Moon similarly placed favour political
science, psychology and metaphysics. Sun-Mercury
combination favours proficiency in mathematics. Venus in
